cards.title: Formalization & momentum
cards.description: Vision Without Borders incorporated in Colorado and submitted its federal tax-exempt application. Clinical instruction at Stanford, global-health fellowship work, and international teaching continued alongside the organization’s launch.
cards.imageAlt: Clinicians working together during international fieldwork

cards.title: Storytelling becomes research
cards.description: An ARVO poster documented the pilot’s early reach: nine global-ophthalmology posts generated 53,000 impressions, with 71% coming from non-followers. The Northern California Screening Project brought the local-access mission into practice.
cards.imageAlt: A clinician conducting an eye examination

cards.title: Learning with Fundación Visión
cards.description: Work in Paraguay focused on preventable-blindness programs and the systems that allow locally grounded care to reach farther.
cards.imageAlt: Clinicians speaking with a community member in Paraguay

cards.title: Specialist care—and systems that remain
cards.description: At the Rwanda International Institute of Ophthalmology, clinical work was paired with a uveitis teaching curriculum designed to support residents long after a visiting specialist leaves.
cards.imageAlt: A child receiving an eye examination in Rwanda

cards.title: The first Aspen fundraiser
cards.description: A community gathering brought the story of preventable blindness home and gave early supporters a direct way to help shape what came next.
cards.imageAlt: Aspen gondolas moving through fresh snow

cards.title: Clinical exchange across Aravind
cards.description: Work with residents at Aravind Eye Hospital in Madurai, Salem, and Pondicherry centered on clinical exchange and shared learning.
cards.imageAlt: Patient education at Aravind Eye Hospital

cards.title: Nepal
cards.description: Clinical work and high-volume outreach brought together surgical care, local expertise, and lessons from an international network of collaborators.
cards.imageAlt: A surgical team during outreach work in Nepal

cards.title: A pilot—and a public story
cards.description: The early pilot ran alongside clinical instruction, resident surgical teaching, and planning for the Northern California Screening Project. A storytelling model began translating global ophthalmology into accessible public narratives.

cards.title: Global ophthalmology work in Ethiopia
cards.description: Following the Global Ophthalmology Summit in Chicago, clinical work began in Addis Ababa—an early foundation for the approach that would become Vision Without Borders.
cards.imageAlt: Global ophthalmology work in Addis Ababa, Ethiopia

cards.title: From one-way to two-way exchange
cards.description: A formal exchange was established between the UCSF Proctor Foundation and Chulalongkorn University—an annual relationship built around knowledge moving in both directions.
cards.imageAlt: Clinicians together during an international clinical exchange

cards.title: Changing corneal-donation rules
cards.description: Research showed that a five-year corneal-donation deferral lacked scientific basis. More than 50 lawmakers cited the data, and the FDA changed its guidance in 2022.
cards.imageAlt: An eye examination during community clinical work

cards.title: Training designed for the community
cards.description: Spanish-language eye-health curricula supported residency training, while cross-sector partnerships helped bring screenings to workers who could otherwise be left out of care.
cards.imageAlt: Clinical teaching and exchange in an eye hospital

cards.title: Technology changes what is possible
cards.description: Smartphone-based imaging and ophthalmology workshops tested how training, validation, and screening tools could expand specialist knowledge beyond a single institution.
cards.imageAlt: A clinician using eye-health technology during an assessment

cards.title: From outcomes to mechanisms
cards.description: Fellowship and research work examined trachoma, the gut microbiome, and infectious blindness while connecting field outcomes to the biological mechanisms behind them.
cards.imageAlt: Two members of a clinical team working together in Ethiopia
